_doc: 9 picks from briefs 190 + 193 where the failure mode is mwcc
register-allocation or expression-scheduling drift. Cluster B
Cluster E = brief 193 PR #640 reg-alloc drift (#7/#8/#12/#13/#14).
- id          : human label (e.g. 'B-08' for brief 190 pick #8).
- address     : hex VA of the function.
- hint        : free-text routing / recipe hint for the decomper.
Neither brief 190 nor brief 193 left .c stubs for these picks
(they attempted-and-reverted or skipped outright). The brief 196
wrapper expects pre-staged stubs at src/<module>/func_<addr>.c;
picks without stubs surface as 'stub_missing' in the result
summary. Brief 198+ (decomper) creates stubs based on the wall

picks.name: func_0205da2c
picks.hint: 1 callee. Cluster B = mwcc 2.0 elides what orig emits. Reg-choice + unused-local elision class.

picks.name: func_01ff8770
picks.hint: 2 callees. ITCM-resident; check whether permuter's compile.sh handles ITCM include path correctly.

picks.name: func_0200b0c8
picks.hint: Brief 098 listed this as the P-4 control (expected ZERO recovery; brief 091/093 ruled out). Useful as sanity gate that the wrapper terminates cleanly on a known-unmatched pick.

picks.name: func_ov011_021d2ca8
picks.hint: 1 callee. Overlay-resident; cross-overlay BL surface unknown — confirm via relocs.txt before staging.

picks.name: func_02023f7c
picks.hint: Cluster E = mwcc reg-alloc drift. Per brief 193 PR #640: orig loads data pointer into r4 (callee-saved) referencing the literal twice; mwcc 2.0/sp1p5 loads into r1 (caller-saved) and CSEs the two literal refs into one.

picks.name: func_02026fd8
picks.hint: Per brief 193 PR #640: clone-of-#7 — same reg-alloc drift class. A converged recipe for E-07 likely applies here.

picks.name: func_02024574
picks.hint: Cluster E reg-alloc drift, distinct shape from E-07 family.

picks.name: func_020270d0
picks.hint: Per brief 193 PR #640: clone-of-#12. Same recipe pool as E-12.

picks.name: func_02028790
picks.hint: Per brief 193 PR #640: clone-of-#12. Same recipe pool as E-12.
